about.name: Varshith Tipirneni
about.title: AI Engineering Student | Building RAG Systems, LLM Pipelines & AI Agents
about.tagline: I don't just call AI APIs — I build the pipelines behind them.
about.summary: Varshith is an AI engineering student actively building a portfolio of production-grade AI systems to land a full-time AI engineering role. He specializes in RAG architecture, LLM evaluation, multi-agent orchestration, and full-stack AI application development. Every project he ships is end-to-end: data ingestion, backend pipeline, evaluation harness, and deployed demo.
about.status: Actively seeking full-time AI engineering roles. Open to SWE roles with an AI/ML focus.
about.philosophy: Build it properly or don't build it at all. Production failure modes, evaluation metrics, and system observability are non-negotiable — not afterthoughts. Each project is designed to demonstrate not just that an AI system works, but that it can be measured, debugged, and trusted.

education.degree: Bachelor's / Graduate-level coursework in Computer Science
highlights: Focused on AI/ML systems, NLP, and software engineering
Self-directed 8-week AI engineering roadmap: 8 hrs/day covering LLM systems, vector databases, RAG, multi-agent frameworks, and cloud deployment
Completed Week 4-5: LLM observability, function calling, prompt engineering patterns, context window management, systems design for AI at scale

impact_bullets: Built 5 end-to-end AI systems from scratch: RAG classifier, research QA pipeline, hallucination benchmark, red-teaming framework, and predictive sports app
Deployed two projects to production: HybridRAG on HuggingFace Spaces + Neon PostgreSQL; portfolio at Netlify
Wrote production-grade security layers: prompt injection detection (14 patterns), rate limiting, CORS, input sanitization
Implemented and evaluated 6 prompt engineering techniques on TruthfulQA; achieved 60pp hallucination reduction with best technique

projects.name: HybridRAG
projects.description: A production-grade pharma MLR claim compliance classifier that uses hybrid retrieval-augmented generation to classify marketing claims as supported, partially supported, off-label, or insufficient. Built for the pharmaceutical/life sciences domain where regulatory accuracy is critical.
projects.problem: Pharmaceutical marketing claims must comply with FDA MLR (Medical, Legal, Regulatory) standards. Manual review is slow and inconsistent. HybridRAG automates claim classification with explainable AI.
projects.architecture: FastAPI backend with Gemini Flash LLM. Data pipeline: FDA OPDP scraper → SHA-256 dedup → embedding generation → Neon PostgreSQL with pgvector. Retrieval quality gate ensures classification only proceeds when retrieved context is sufficiently relevant. RAGAS evaluation harness (5 metrics) for offline and live eval.
key_features: SHA-256 deduplication to prevent re-ingesting scraped data
Retrieval quality gate: rejects low-confidence retrievals before LLM call
Prompt injection detection with 14 pattern checks
Rate limiting and CORS-locked endpoints
RAGAS evaluation: context precision, recall, faithfulness, answer relevancy, and context entity recall
GitHub Actions CI/CD for automated ingestion
projects.results: Deployed to HuggingFace Spaces + Neon. Fresh eval (30 cases): 40% accuracy — off_label and insufficient_data labels identified as needing knowledge base improvement. Eval results logged for continuous improvement.
resume_bullets: Built pharma MLR claim compliance classifier using RAG with Gemini Flash; deployed to HuggingFace Spaces + Neon PostgreSQL
Implemented production security: 14-pattern prompt injection guard, rate limiter, SHA-256 dedup pipeline
Evaluated with RAGAS (5 metrics) across 30 fresh cases; identified label imbalance for iterative KB improvement

projects.name: PRISM
projects.status: Phase 2 complete — tested on real arXiv papers
projects.description: A multi-document research intelligence pipeline that ingests arXiv papers, builds a hybrid retrieval index (BM25 + dense), generates structured intelligence briefs, and detects contradictions across papers. Built for researchers and analysts who need to synthesize findings across multiple sources quickly.
projects.problem: Reading and synthesizing 5-10 research papers for a literature review takes hours. PRISM does it in seconds: fetch papers, extract key findings, identify conflicts, generate a structured brief.
projects.architecture: Phase 1: single-document QA (fetch → parse → chunk → embed → store → answer). Phase 2: multi-document with hybrid BM25 + Qdrant dense retrieval, RRF fusion, structured brief generation (Groq JSON mode), cross-document conflict detection. Local Qdrant vector store.
key_features: Hybrid retrieval: BM25 (keyword) + dense (semantic) with Reciprocal Rank Fusion
Overlapping window chunker (chunk_size=1000, overlap=200) to preserve context across boundaries
Structured intelligence brief: Groq JSON mode with max_tokens=4096
Cross-document conflict detector: flags contradicting findings across papers
Multi-document ingestion with deduplication
Local RAGAS metrics: context_overlap, token_f1, exact_match
projects.results: Phase 2 tested against real arXiv papers. Hybrid retrieval demonstrably outperforms dense-only on follow-up questions and multi-hop reasoning.
resume_bullets: Built multi-document research QA pipeline with BM25+dense hybrid retrieval (RRF fusion) over local Qdrant; tested on real arXiv papers
Implemented cross-document conflict detection and structured brief generation using Groq JSON mode

projects.name: HallucinationBenchmark
projects.description: A systematic benchmark evaluating 6 prompt engineering techniques for reducing hallucination in Claude models (Haiku and Sonnet) on TruthfulQA. Uses a custom LLM-as-judge (Claude Sonnet via DeepEval) instead of OpenAI, tracked in MLflow.
projects.problem: LLM hallucination rates vary widely based on prompting strategy. Which technique works best, for which model, and in which question categories? This benchmark answers it with data.
projects.architecture: 6 experiment configs (baseline, few-shot, CoT, JSON mode, system-prompt-v1, system-prompt-v2) × 2 models (Haiku, Sonnet) on TruthfulQA MCQ1. DeepEval HallucinationMetric with ClaudeJudge. Results tracked in MLflow (SQLite backend). Per-category analysis across 38 categories.
key_features: Custom ClaudeJudge replacing OpenAI judge in DeepEval
6 prompt engineering techniques evaluated systematically
Per-category hallucination rates (38 categories from TruthfulQA)
MLflow experiment tracking with full reproducibility
Interactive HTML dashboard with real data
projects.results: Best technique: system-prompt-v2 → 39.7% hallucination rate, a 60.3pp reduction vs baseline (Haiku: 18.4%, Sonnet: 15.4% baseline). Key finding: Chain-of-Thought made Superstitions category worse. Temperature 0.3 produced 0pp improvement. Results dashboard deployed as HTML.
resume_bullets: Benchmarked 6 prompt engineering techniques on TruthfulQA across 38 categories; system-prompt-v2 achieved 60pp hallucination reduction
Built custom LLM-as-judge (ClaudeJudge) replacing OpenAI in DeepEval; tracked all experiments in MLflow

projects.name: RedTeamAgent
projects.description: A LangGraph-based multi-agent red-teaming system that automatically discovers vulnerabilities in LLM-powered chatbots. A victim HR chatbot (Groq/Llama 3.1) faces 5 specialized attack agents (prompt injection, jailbreak, data extraction, goal hijack, hallucination), scored by a Claude Sonnet judge.
projects.problem: LLM systems have security vulnerabilities that are hard to discover manually. RedTeamAgent automates adversarial probing across 5 attack categories, provides quantified vulnerability scores, and drives adaptive escalation when defenses are detected.
projects.architecture: Two services: victim_chatbot (FastAPI, Groq/Llama 3.1 8B, 4-layer defense: input patterns + hardened system prompt + output scan + turn drift) and red_team_agent (LangGraph orchestrator, 5 attack agents, Claude Sonnet generator + judge, weighted vulnerability scoring with canary breach floor). Dashboard with per-layer defense toggles.
key_features: 5 specialized attack agents: PromptInjection, Jailbreak, DataExtraction, GoalHijack, Hallucination
Claude Sonnet judge evaluates output harm (not just technique bypass)
Canary tokens in knowledge base — breach floors vulnerability score at 7.0
Round 2 adaptive escalation based on Round 1 failures
Live dashboard with 4 defense layer toggles
LangGraph state machine: run_attack_agents → score → escalate → report
projects.results: Core red-teaming loop working. Victim chatbot successfully switched from Claude to Groq so defense layers have measurable effect. Layer 2 (hardened system prompt) toggle added. Next: all-layers-off vs all-layers-on comparison for portfolio story.
resume_bullets: Built LangGraph multi-agent red-teaming system with 5 attack agents, Claude Sonnet judge, and adaptive escalation
Implemented 4-layer defense pipeline (input filter, hardened prompt, output scan, turn drift) with live dashboard toggles

projects.name: WorldCup2026Predictor
projects.description: A full-stack World Cup 2026 prediction app with Monte Carlo simulation, bracket visualization, and a live match center. Built end-to-end in a single sprint: 45 tickets from scratch to working app.
projects.architecture: React frontend (Vite) + FastAPI backend + SQLite. Monte Carlo match simulation, bracket progression logic, live match polling engine (minute-by-minute replay), FIFA-style Match Center page.
key_features: Monte Carlo simulation for match outcome prediction
Live match center with 3-second polling and event ticker
Full bracket visualization (group stage through final)
16 backend tests, all green

faq.a: Yes — Varshith is actively seeking full-time AI engineering roles. He's especially interested in positions involving RAG systems, LLM pipelines, AI agents, or applied ML. He's available immediately and based in the Eastern time zone.

faq.q: What makes Varshith different from other AI engineering candidates?
faq.a: Most candidates call AI APIs. Varshith builds the infrastructure around them: custom RAG pipelines with hybrid retrieval, evaluation harnesses with real metrics, multi-agent orchestration with LangGraph, and production security layers. Every project ships with an eval story and failure mode analysis — not just a working demo.

faq.q: What is Varshith's preferred tech stack?
faq.a: Python + FastAPI for the backend, Groq or Gemini for the LLM, Qdrant for vector search, and GitHub Actions for CI/CD. For multi-agent work: LangGraph. For evaluation: RAGAS and DeepEval. He's comfortable adapting to any stack a team already uses.

faq.q: What types of AI engineering roles is Varshith targeting?
faq.a: AI Engineer, LLM Engineer, ML Engineer, Applied AI Engineer, or Software Engineer with a strong AI/ML focus. He's interested in teams building LLM-powered products, internal AI tooling, or AI infrastructure.

faq.q: Has Varshith deployed anything to production?
faq.a: Yes. HybridRAG is live on HuggingFace Spaces with Neon PostgreSQL as the backend database. His portfolio is deployed to Netlify. Both include CI/CD pipelines via GitHub Actions.

Deep. He built HybridRAG (pharma MLR classifier with retrieval quality gates and RAGAS evaluation) and PRISM (multi-document research pipeline with BM25 + dense hybrid retrieval and RRF fusion). He understands the full pipeline: chunking strategy, embedding models, vector store selection, retrieval quality metrics, and LLM grounding. ARIA — the chatbot you're using right now — is also a RAG system he built.
Email: varshith.tipirneni@gmail.com. You can also find him on LinkedIn and GitHub (links in his portfolio). He responds quickly.

faq.q: What is Varshith currently working on?
faq.a: He just finished WorldCup2026Predictor (all 45 tickets complete) and is building ARIA — this chatbot. He's also partway through RedTeamAgent. His roadmap prioritizes shipping portfolio-quality projects that demonstrate end-to-end AI engineering skills.

faq.q: What LLM evaluation experience does Varshith have?
faq.a: Extensive. He built a systematic hallucination benchmark across 6 prompt engineering techniques on TruthfulQA (38 categories, 2 Claude models) with a custom LLM-as-judge. He uses RAGAS for RAG evaluation (5 metrics). He understands the difference between online and offline eval, and how to build reproducible eval harnesses with MLflow.

faq.q: Tell me about Varshith's security engineering experience
faq.a: In HybridRAG, he built a production security layer from scratch: 14-pattern prompt injection detection, IP-based rate limiting, input sanitization, and CORS locking. In RedTeamAgent, he built the other side — adversarial attack agents that probe for those same vulnerabilities. This gives him an unusually complete picture of LLM system security.
